front: Composer: German-born French composer; most famous for the can-can ('Infernal Galop') from his operetta and for The Tales of Hoffmann
Composer: Manuel Rosenthal arranged his pieces for the ballet Gaîté Parisienne; Josephine Baker starred in a 1934 revival of his opera La Créole
Opera: satirical parody of Gluck's Orpheus; Eurydice hates Orpheus and runs away with Jupiter; Public Opinion forces the rescue
work: Orpheus in the Underworld (Orphée aux enfers)

front: Opera: 'Infernal Galop' became the definitive can-can tune; Saint-Saëns slowed it enormously for the 'Tortoises' movement of Carnival of the Animals
work: Orpheus in the Underworld (Orphée aux enfers)

front: Opera: Jupiter appears in the 'Fly Duet' ('Duo de la mouche') disguised as a fly, singing only buzzing noises
work: Orpheus in the Underworld (Orphée aux enfers)

front: Opera: libretto by Ludovic Halévy (whose uncle Fromental wrote The Jewess)
work: Orpheus in the Underworld (Orphée aux enfers)

front: Opera: US Marines' Hymn is based on the 'Gendarmes' Duet' from this composer's Geneviève de Brabant
work: Orpheus in the Underworld (Orphée aux enfers)

front: Opera: three acts named after Hoffmann's three loves — Olympia, Antonia, and Giulietta; Prologue/Epilogue set in Luther's Tavern; left unfinished, completed by Ernest Guiraud
work: The Tales of Hoffmann (Les contes d'Hoffmann)

front: Opera: Olympia act based on E.T.A. Hoffmann's 'The Sandman'; Hoffmann falls for Spalanzani's mechanical doll
work: The Tales of Hoffmann (Les contes d'Hoffmann)

front: Opera: Dr. Miracle causes Antonia's death by encouraging her to sing (Act 2)
work: The Tales of Hoffmann (Les contes d'Hoffmann)

front: Opera: Giulietta seduces Hoffmann to steal his reflection (Act 3, Venice); 1951 Powell and Pressburger film starred Robert Rounseville and Moira Shearer
work: The Tales of Hoffmann (Les contes d'Hoffmann)

front: Opera: famous Venetian barcarolle sung by Giulietta and Nicklausse in Act 3 of The Tales of Hoffmann
back: "Belle nuit, o nuit d'amour" from The Tales of Hoffmann (Offenbach)

front: Opera: coloratura soprano aria sung by Olympia the mechanical doll; she periodically slows to a halt and needs to be re-wound; French title = 'The birds in the arbor'
back: "Les oiseaux dans la charmille" (Doll Song) from The Tales of Hoffmann (Offenbach)
work: "Les oiseaux dans la charmille" (Doll Song / Doll Aria)
